hook.text: The Maya independently invented zero—one of only a handful of civilizations to do so.

buildup.text: Their number system was base 20, using dots for ones and bars for fives.

discovery.text: A shell symbol represented zero. The Maya used it as a true placeholder in calculations.

twist.text: Their calendar required astronomical precision. Math wasn't abstract—it tracked the cosmos.

climax.text: Mayan astronomers predicted eclipses centuries in advance using this base-20 arithmetic.

punchline.text: A shell-shaped zero helped predict eclipses centuries ahead.

quiz.question: What base did the Mayan number system use?
